meta_title: For Students: project management to Solve can't scale projects (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to project management and overcome can't scale projects. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Project Management for Students: Solve Scaling Bottlenecks
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with unscalable code. Learn how to master project management using Antigravity and Claude Code to build professional-grade SaaS applications.
content.answer_block: Scaling issues often stem from poor architecture, not just code volume. By implementing rigorous project management frameworks and leveraging AI-driven CLI tools like Claude Code, you can decompose complex features into modular, manageable components, ensuring your codebase remains extensible, clean, and production-ready as you scale toward your first launch.
content.intro:
You’ve spent weeks crafting your first SaaS project, but as soon as you add a third feature, the house of cards begins to wobble. Your code is a labyrinth, your file structure is a mystery, and adding a simple button feels like performing open-heart surgery on a running engine. You aren't failing; you've simply hit the 'Intermediate Wall' where raw coding skill meets the cold reality of architectural entropy.

True engineering isn't just about writing functions; it's about managing complexity before it manages you. By adopting professional project management workflows and integrating automated AI assistance, you transform from a coder who 'gets it working' into an architect who builds systems that last. Let’s refactor your approach to project management and get your app ready for real-world usage.

steps.title: Define Architectural Boundaries
steps.description: Before writing code, define your module boundaries. This prevents the 'spaghetti' effect where every file depends on every other file.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current project structure and suggest a modular directory architecture that separates business logic, API routes, and UI components.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal --prompt 'Map my current file structure and identify circular dependencies that hinder scaling.'

steps.title: Implement a Task-Oriented Workflow
steps.description: Break down your feature list into atomic tasks. This allows you to track progress and prevent feature creep.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Convert my feature list into a structured JSON tasks file with dependencies and estimated complexity levels.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal --prompt 'Create a tasks.json file based on my codebase requirements and prioritize them by technical dependency.'

steps.title: Automate Documentation Generation
steps.description: If your code isn't documented, it isn't scalable. Use AI to generate technical specs as you build.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a README.md and technical design document based on the current project directory structure.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal --prompt 'Generate comprehensive documentation for all exported modules in the src/ folder.'

steps.title: Enforce Strict Type Safety
steps.description: Scaling requires predictability. Move to a strictly typed environment to catch bugs before they hit production.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Identify areas in my codebase where type safety is missing and suggest interfaces to improve maintainability.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal --prompt 'Apply TypeScript interfaces to all API response objects in the current project.'

steps.title: Establish an Automated Testing Loop
steps.description: You cannot scale what you cannot verify. Integrate automated tests to ensure new features don't break old ones.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write unit tests for the core business logic modules identified in step 1 using Vitest or Jest.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal --prompt 'Generate a test suite for the authentication module and verify it with the current codebase.'

pro_tips: Treat your project documentation as 'code'—if it isn't in the repo, it doesn't exist.
Use feature flags to decouple deployment from release, allowing you to scale features incrementally.
Always refactor immediately after a feature is finished; technical debt compounds at an exponential rate.
Leverage Git branching strategies even for solo projects to maintain a clean production history.
Automate your CI/CD pipeline early; manual deployment is the biggest bottleneck to scaling.
content.next_steps: Once you have successfully refactored your current project, deploy a staging environment and implement a continuous integration pipeline to automate your testing and deployment workflows.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools and the blueprint to stop building toys and start building software. The gap between a student project and a professional SaaS is consistency, architecture, and the right workflow. Don't wait for your code to break—start managing your complexity today. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
